articles.headline: Supreme Court Mandates 'Green Tribunal Plus' Model for Enhanced Environmental Enforcement
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has directed the Union Government to establish a 'Green Tribunal Plus' model, expanding the powers and jurisdiction of the National Green Tribunal (NGT).
• This new model aims to streamline environmental clearances, expedite dispute resolution, and ensure stricter compliance with environmental laws across states.
• The ruling emphasizes a proactive role for the NGT in monitoring project implementation and imposing penalties for violations, moving beyond a purely adjudicatory function.
• It calls for enhanced technical expertise within the NGT and a dedicated environmental police force to enforce its directives.
• The judgment comes amidst growing concerns over environmental degradation and the slow pace of justice in complex ecological cases.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Environmental protection acts and policies
prelimsPoints: National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 under the National Green Tribunal Act.
NGT has jurisdiction over all civil cases involving substantial questions relating to the environment.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' and 'Precautionary Principle' are fundamental principles applied by the NGT.
The Supreme Court's ruling expands NGT's enforcement and monitoring capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in environmental protection and the challenges of environmental governance in India.
Analyze the implications of the 'Green Tribunal Plus' model for environmental compliance and sustainable development.
Critically evaluate the balance between economic development and environmental conservation in the context of judicial interventions.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting the scales of justice balancing environment and development, with the NGT logo in the background.
articles.trivia: The NGT was the third such institution in the world, after Australia and New Zealand, and the first developing country to establish a dedicated environmental tribunal.

articles.headline: Economic Survey 2024-25 Projects Robust Growth Amidst Global Headwinds; Focus on Green Transition
articles.explainer:
• The Economic Survey 2024-25, tabled ahead of the Union Budget, projects India's GDP growth to remain robust at 7.0-7.5% for the next fiscal year.
• It highlights strong domestic demand, government capital expenditure, and increased private investment as key growth drivers.
• The survey emphasizes the need for a sustained push towards green energy transition and climate-resilient infrastructure to achieve long-term sustainable growth.
• It also points to global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and commodity price volatility as potential risks.
• Recommendations include further reforms in agriculture, labour markets, and a rationalization of subsidies for fiscal consolidation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is presented annually by the Ministry of Finance, typically before the Union Budget.
It provides a detailed review of the economy's performance and policy recommendations.
Key economic indicators like GDP growth, inflation, and fiscal deficit are discussed.
The survey's focus on green transition aligns with India's climate commitments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers and challenges for India's economic growth as highlighted by the Economic Survey 2024-25.
Discuss the importance of green transition and climate-resilient infrastructure in achieving sustainable economic development.
Evaluate the government's economic policies in light of global headwinds and recommend further reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing a rising growth arrow superimposed with green energy symbols, representing economic growth and green transition.
articles.trivia: The first Economic Survey was presented in 1950-51, and until 1964, it was presented along with the Union Budget.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Enhanced Cooperation in Defence, Space, and Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership and charting new avenues for cooperation.
• Both nations committed to expanding defence industrial collaboration, including co-development and co-production of advanced military platforms.
• Significant progress was made in space cooperation, with agreements on joint satellite missions and sharing of satellite data for climate monitoring.
• The dialogue also focused on enhancing maritime security and stability in the Indo-Pacific region through joint exercises and intelligence sharing.
• Discussions covered global and regional issues, including counter-terrorism, cyber security, and clean energy transition.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership established in 1998.
Key areas of cooperation include defence, space, civil nuclear energy, and counter-terrorism.
France has been a crucial partner for India in its strategic autonomy goals.
The Indo-Pacific strategy is a shared priority for both nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in India's foreign policy and its role in global affairs.
Discuss how enhanced cooperation in defence and space contributes to India's strategic capabilities and national security.
Evaluate the importance of India-France collaboration in maintaining stability and promoting a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific region.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France depicted against a backdrop of a satellite and a fighter jet, symbolizing strategic cooperation.
articles.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a Strategic Partnership with India, demonstrating a deep trust and convergence of interests.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Cryogenic Engine for Future Deep Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful hot test of its next-generation cryogenic engine, a crucial step for future deep space missions.
• This advanced engine, designated 'Vikas-II', boasts higher thrust-to-weight ratio and improved specific impulse, enhancing payload capacity and mission duration.
• The technology is vital for India's ambitious interplanetary missions, including potential Venus orbiter and advanced lunar exploration programs.
• The successful test signifies India's growing self-reliance in cutting-edge space propulsion technology, reducing dependence on foreign components.
• It also paves the way for heavier launch vehicles capable of deploying multiple satellites into diverse orbits.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants at very low temperatures.
ISRO's GSLV MkIII (LVM3) uses a cryogenic upper stage.
The 'Vikas' engine family is a staple in ISRO's launch vehicles.
Successful testing of new engines is critical for advancing India's space capabilities.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of indigenous cryogenic engine technology for India's space program and its strategic implications.
Discuss how advancements in space propulsion technology contribute to India's aspirations for deep space exploration and international collaboration.
Analyze the role of ISRO in fostering scientific temper and technological innovation in India.
articles.imageDescription: Diagram of a cryogenic engine undergoing a test fire, with the ISRO logo prominently displayed.
articles.trivia: India became the sixth nation in the world to develop cryogenic engine technology, a complex and challenging feat in aerospace engineering.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' Blueprint, Emphasizing Digital Governance and Social Equity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today unveiled a comprehensive 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint, outlining India's development trajectory for the next 22 years.
• The vision document focuses on transforming India into a developed nation by 2047, with pillars of digital governance, social equity, sustainable growth, and global leadership.
• Key initiatives include expanding digital public infrastructure, ensuring universal access to quality education and healthcare, and promoting inclusive economic opportunities.
• It also stresses on strengthening democratic institutions, fostering innovation, and enhancing India's soft power globally.
• The blueprint calls for collaborative efforts from states, private sector, and civil society to achieve these ambitious goals.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Governance, Transparency and Accountability
prelimsPoints: Amrit Kaal refers to the 25-year period from India's 75th Independence Day (2022) to the 100th (2047).
The vision document sets long-term goals for India's socio-economic development.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) includes platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker.
Focus on social equity aims to reduce disparities and ensure opportunities for all.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint and its potential impact on India's development trajectory.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing digital governance and ensuring social equity across diverse regions of India.
Critically evaluate the role of various stakeholders, including government, private sector, and civil society, in achieving the goals of Vision 2047.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract graphic depicting India's growth trajectory towards 2047, with elements of technology, sustainability, and diverse communities.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Amrit Kaal' was first introduced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2021, signifying a period of great opportunities.

articles.headline: New National Wetlands Conservation Plan Launched to Combat Degradation and Boost Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a comprehensive National Wetlands Conservation Plan (NWCP) 2.0.
• The plan aims to strengthen the protection and management of wetlands across India, which are crucial for biodiversity, water security, and climate resilience.
• Key features include a national inventory of wetlands, capacity building for local communities, and integration of wetland conservation into regional planning.
• It also focuses on adopting nature-based solutions for wetland restoration and promoting sustainable livelihoods dependent on these ecosystems.
• The initiative is a response to the alarming rate of wetland degradation due to urbanization, pollution, and unsustainable agricultural practices.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year.
Wetlands provide various ecosystem services like water purification, flood control, and habitat for wildlife.
India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands.
National Wetlands Conservation Programme (NWCP) was initiated in 1985-86.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the major threats they face in India.
Analyze the features and potential effectiveness of the new National Wetlands Conservation Plan in achieving its objectives.
Suggest measures to integrate wetland conservation into broader sustainable development goals and enhance community participation.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of a vibrant wetland ecosystem, showcasing diverse flora and fauna, with a small village in the background.
articles.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar sites, covering a significant area of its wetlands, making it one of the largest networks in Asia.

articles.headline: India Enhances Border Infrastructure Along Northern Fronts with Advanced Surveillance Systems
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a significant upgrade in border infrastructure along India's northern frontiers, deploying advanced surveillance and communication systems.
• The initiative includes the construction of strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels to facilitate faster troop movement and logistics in challenging terrains.
• New-generation drones, integrated sensor networks, and AI-powered monitoring stations are being installed to enhance real-time situational awareness.
• This move is aimed at bolstering national security, deterring incursions, and improving the operational readiness of armed forces in critical sectors.
• The government reiterates its commitment to safeguarding India's territorial integrity and sovereignty.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: India shares land borders with seven countries, with the northern borders being particularly sensitive.
Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Advanced surveillance systems include drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors.
The push for infrastructure aims to counter challenges posed by difficult terrain and adversarial actions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly along its northern frontiers.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing and maintaining infrastructure in high-altitude and difficult border regions.
Examine how technological advancements in surveillance and communication can enhance border management and deterrence capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Army personnel patrolling a newly constructed border road in a snowy mountainous region, with surveillance equipment visible.
articles.trivia: The Atal Tunnel (Rohtang Tunnel), inaugurated in 2020, significantly reduced travel time to key border areas in Himachal Pradesh and Ladakh.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Boost Domestic Manufacturing in Advanced Materials Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved the expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include the Advanced Materials sector.
• This strategic move aims to incentivize domestic and global manufacturers to set up production units for critical advanced materials in India.
• The scheme will focus on materials crucial for sectors like semiconductors, advanced batteries, specialty chemicals, and aerospace components.
• The expansion is expected to reduce India's import dependence, create high-skill jobs, and integrate India into global value chains for cutting-edge technologies.
• This aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, fostering self-reliance in strategic industries.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Investment models
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme offers incentives to companies for increasing production in specific sectors.
It was initially launched to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.
Advanced materials are critical for high-tech industries and national security.
The scheme aims to attract foreign investment and encourage domestic players.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind expanding the PLI scheme to the Advanced Materials sector and its potential economic benefits for India.
Discuss how the PLI scheme contributes to India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and its integration into global supply chains for critical technologies.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing a robust domestic manufacturing ecosystem for advanced materials in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image showing gears and microchips, representing industrial growth and advanced technology, with the 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme has been extended to over a dozen sectors, including electronics, automobiles, pharmaceuticals, and textiles, since its inception.

articles.headline: National Skill Development Mission 2.0 to Focus on Future-Ready Workforce and Green Skills
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced the launch of National Skill Development Mission (NSDM) 2.0.
• This revamped mission aims to equip India's youth with future-ready skills, focusing on emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and data science.
• A significant emphasis will be placed on 'green skills' to support India's transition to a sustainable economy and meet climate goals.
• The mission will integrate vocational training with formal education, ensuring better employability and career progression for skilled individuals.
• It also seeks to foster entrepreneurship and create a robust ecosystem for lifelong learning and skill upgradation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Skill Development Mission was launched in 2015 to create convergence across skill development schemes.
India has a large youth population, presenting a demographic dividend.
Green skills are competencies required to work in green economy sectors or to green existing jobs.
Integration of vocational training with formal education is a key recommendation of NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in harnessing India's demographic dividend and addressing the challenges of unemployment.
Analyze how the focus on future-ready and green skills under NSDM 2.0 can contribute to India's economic growth and sustainable development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in bridging the skill gap and promoting entrepreneurship among youth.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse group of young people learning various skills, including digital literacy and practical trade, in a modern training center.
articles.trivia: India aims to skill over 400 million people by 2022 under various skill development programs, though progress remains a challenge.

articles.headline: India Pledges Stronger Role in Global South Climate Action at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India's representative at the ongoing UN Climate Forum reiterated the nation's commitment to lead climate action for the Global South.
• India pledged enhanced support for technology transfer, capacity building, and climate finance for developing countries.
• The statement called for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments and provide accessible green technologies.
• Emphasis was placed on promoting sustainable lifestyles and advocating for 'climate justice' at international platforms.
• India highlighted its own progress in renewable energy expansion and adaptation strategies, offering to share best practices.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies.
India has been a strong advocate for 'Climate Justice' and 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities'.
UN Climate Forums (COPs) are annual conferences under the UNFCCC.
India's renewable energy capacity has significantly increased in recent years.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in advocating for the concerns of the Global South regarding climate change at international forums.
Analyze the concept of 'climate justice' and 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities' in the context of global climate negotiations.
Evaluate the challenges faced by developing countries in achieving climate goals and the role of international cooperation in addressing them.
articles.imageDescription: Representatives from various nations gathered at a UN climate conference, with a focus on developing countries.
articles.trivia: India launched the International Solar Alliance (ISA) in 2015, a treaty-based intergovernmental organization, primarily to promote solar energy among member countries.

articles.headline: AI-Powered Healthcare Diagnostics Pilot Project Launched in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has launched a pilot project integrating AI-powered diagnostic tools in primary healthcare centers in select rural districts.
• The initiative aims to improve early detection of diseases like tuberculosis, diabetes, and certain cancers, especially in underserved areas.
• AI algorithms will assist medical professionals in analyzing scans, pathology reports, and patient data for quicker and more accurate diagnoses.
• The project also includes training local healthcare workers to operate and interpret results from these new technologies.
• This move is part of the broader 'Digital India Healthcare' push to leverage technology for equitable healthcare access.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
prelimsPoints: AI is a branch of computer science that aims to create intelligent machines.
Primary Healthcare Centers (PHCs) are the first point of contact for healthcare in rural areas.
Telemedicine involves providing clinical healthcare at a distance using technology.
Digital India Healthcare mission aims to build a comprehensive digital health ecosystem.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing healthcare delivery, particularly in rural and underserved regions of India.
Analyze the challenges and ethical considerations associated with integrating AI into healthcare diagnostics and patient care.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access to advanced healthcare technologies and prevent the digital divide in health services.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor in a rural clinic using an AI-assisted diagnostic device, with a patient in the background.
articles.trivia: India is home to one of the largest digital public health initiatives globally, leveraging technology for vaccination drives and health record management.

articles.headline: Proposed Amendments to RTI Act Spark Debate on Transparency and Accountability
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has introduced proposed amendments to the Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005, sparking a nationwide debate.
• Critics argue that certain proposed changes could dilute the independence of Information Commissions and restrict access to crucial government data.
• Proponents, however, contend that the amendments aim to rationalize the functioning of the Act, address misuse, and ensure better alignment with other legal frameworks.
• Civil society organizations and opposition parties have expressed strong concerns, calling for greater public consultation and parliamentary scrutiny.
• The debate highlights the ongoing tension between government transparency and administrative efficiency.
gsSubTopics: Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
Citizens Charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: The Right to Information Act, 2005, allows citizens to request information from public authorities.
The Central Information Commission (CIC) and State Information Commissions (SICs) are the highest appellate bodies.
The Act aims to promote transparency and accountability in the working of every public authority.
Previous amendments to the RTI Act have also faced criticism regarding the independence of Information Commissioners.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Right to Information Act in strengthening democratic governance and ensuring accountability in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the proposed amendments to the RTI Act, considering their potential impact on transparency and citizen's rights.
Suggest measures to balance the need for government efficiency with the principles of transparency and public access to information.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a magnifying glass over a document titled 'RTI Act', symbolizing scrutiny and transparency, with a blurred Parliament building in the background.
articles.trivia: The RTI Act was enacted after a long and sustained movement led by civil society organizations, with Mazdoor Kisan Shakti Sangathan (MKSS) being a prominent one.

articles.headline: Himalayan Ecosystems Face Unprecedented Threats from Climate Change and Tourism: New Report
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE) highlights the severe threats facing Himalayan ecosystems.
• Climate change, marked by rising temperatures and erratic precipitation, is accelerating glacial melt, impacting water resources and increasing disaster risks.
• Unregulated tourism and infrastructure development are leading to habitat fragmentation, biodiversity loss, and increased waste generation.
• The report calls for urgent implementation of sustainable tourism policies, climate adaptation strategies, and community-led conservation efforts.
• It emphasizes the critical role of the Himalayas as a global biodiversity hotspot and a source of freshwater for billions.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Himalayas are one of the world's most ecologically sensitive and biodiverse regions.
Glacial melt in the Himalayas contributes to river systems like the Indus, Ganga, and Brahmaputra.
Sustainable tourism aims to minimize negative impacts on the environment and local culture.
ICFRE is an autonomous organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted threats posed by climate change and unsustainable human activities to the fragile Himalayan ecosystems.
Analyze the socio-economic and environmental consequences of glacial melt and biodiversity loss in the Himalayan region.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for promoting sustainable tourism, climate adaptation, and community-based conservation in the Indian Himalayas.
articles.imageDescription: Panoramic view of a pristine Himalayan landscape with visible glaciers, contrasting with signs of human development and plastic waste.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas influence India's climate significantly by blocking cold winds from Central Asia and causing monsoon rains on the Indian subcontinent.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Model Gains International Recognition, India to Share Expertise
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, exemplified by Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, has garnered significant international acclaim.
• Several developing nations are keen to adopt or adapt India's DPI framework to boost financial inclusion, digital payments, and e-governance.
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT announced initiatives to facilitate knowledge sharing and technical assistance to countries interested in implementing their own DPIs.
• This recognition positions India as a global leader in digital transformation and a key contributor to digital public goods.
• The success of DPI highlights the potential of technology to drive inclusive growth and empower citizens.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private innovation.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker allows citizens to store digital documents securely.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key components and benefits of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model in fostering financial inclusion and e-governance.
Discuss how India's success in DPI can serve as a model for other developing countries and contribute to global digital transformation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in replicating and scaling DPI solutions while ensuring data privacy and cybersecurity.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, with a world map in the background.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, making it one of the largest real-time payment systems globally.

articles.headline: Coastal Security Enhanced with New Patrol Vessels and Integrated Surveillance Network
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) has commissioned several new advanced offshore patrol vessels (OPVs) and fast patrol vessels (FPVs) to bolster coastal security.
• An integrated coastal surveillance network, linking various sensors, radars, and command centers, has also been expanded along critical stretches.
• This enhancement aims to counter maritime threats such as illegal immigration, smuggling, drug trafficking, and potential terror infiltration via sea routes.
• The initiative involves collaborative efforts with state marine police, Indian Navy, and other stakeholders for a layered defence mechanism.
• It underscores India's commitment to securing its vast coastline and exclusive economic zone (EEZ).
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
prelimsPoints: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km and an EEZ of over 2 million sq km.
The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is responsible for enforcing maritime laws and protecting India's maritime interests.
The 26/11 Mumbai attacks highlighted vulnerabilities in coastal security.
Integrated Coastal Surveillance System (ICSS) aims to provide real-time information on maritime activities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional threats to India's coastal security and the strategic importance of safeguarding its maritime boundaries.
Analyze the measures taken by India to enhance its coastal security apparatus, including technological upgrades and inter-agency coordination.
Evaluate the challenges in managing a vast coastline and suggest further steps to strengthen maritime domain awareness and response capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard patrol vessel cruising along the coastline, with a radar station in the background.
articles.trivia: The Indian Coast Guard was formally established in 1978 under the Coast Guard Act, 1978, as an independent armed force of the Union.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Scheme for Promotion of Traditional Indian Arts and Crafts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has unveiled a new national scheme aimed at revitalizing and promoting traditional Indian arts and crafts.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, market linkages, and training programs for artisans, particularly those from marginalized communities.
• It emphasizes preserving endangered art forms, encouraging innovation while retaining traditional essence, and creating global platforms for Indian craftsmanship.
• Initiatives include setting up 'Heritage Craft Villages' and collaborating with e-commerce platforms for wider reach and fair remuneration.
• This effort seeks to empower artisans, boost rural economies, and showcase India's rich cultural heritage on the world stage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: India has a diverse range of traditional arts and crafts, many with GI tags.
GI tag is used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation that are due to that origin.
Ministry of Culture is responsible for the preservation and promotion of Indian culture.
Promotion of traditional crafts contributes to rural livelihoods and cultural tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian arts and crafts in preserving cultural heritage and contributing to the rural economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian artisans in the contemporary market and how the new scheme aims to address them.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives and technological platforms in promoting Indian craftsmanship globally and enhancing India's soft power.
articles.imageDescription: A skilled artisan meticulously crafting a traditional Indian pottery piece, surrounded by colorful finished products.
articles.trivia: Channapatna toys from Karnataka, Madhubani paintings from Bihar, and Kanchipuram silk sarees from Tamil Nadu are some famous Indian crafts with GI tags.

articles.headline: Lokpal Institution Completes 5 Years, Reviews Progress in Anti-Corruption Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Lokpal of India today marked its fifth anniversary, taking stock of its progress in establishing accountability and combating corruption in public administration.
• The institution highlighted key cases investigated, recommendations made for systemic improvements, and the challenges faced in its initial years.
• The Lokpal emphasized the importance of a robust whistleblower protection mechanism and greater public awareness for effective anti-corruption efforts.
• It called for closer coordination with other anti-corruption agencies and timely disposal of cases to enhance its credibility.
• The review underscored the ongoing journey towards transparent and ethical governance in India.
gsSubTopics: Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of Lokpal for the Union and Lokayukta for States.
Lokpal is an anti-corruption authority or body of ombudsman who represents the public interest.
The first Lokpal of India, Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose, was appointed in 2019.
The institution investigates allegations of corruption against public functionaries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Lokpal institution in promoting transparency and combating corruption in India's public administration.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Lokpal in its functioning and suggest measures to enhance its effectiveness and independence.
Evaluate the broader framework of anti-corruption mechanisms in India and how the Lokpal contributes to ethical governance.
articles.imageDescription: The emblem of the Lokpal of India, symbolizing justice and accountability, against a backdrop of government buildings.
articles.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman originated in Sweden in 1809. India's Lokpal is modeled on the ombudsman system.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Notifies E-Waste Management Rules, 2025, Emphasizing Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified the new E-Waste Management Rules, 2025, to address the growing challenge of electronic waste.
• The updated rules introduce stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets for manufacturers and importers, promoting a circular economy approach.
• They mandate the use of recycled content in new electronic products and establish a robust digital platform for tracking e-waste generation and recycling.
• The rules also aim to formalize the informal e-waste recycling sector, ensuring safe and environmentally sound disposal practices.
• This move is critical for resource conservation, pollution control, and public health, aligning with India's sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: E-waste includes discarded electrical or electronic devices.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) makes producers responsible for the environmental management of their products at end-of-life.
Circular economy aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible, extracting the maximum value from them.
India's first E-waste Management Rules were notified in 2011.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental and health hazards posed by e-waste and the challenges in its management in India.
Analyze the key features of the E-Waste Management Rules, 2025, and their potential to promote a circular economy and sustainable practices.
Suggest measures to enhance public awareness, formalize the informal recycling sector, and ensure effective implementation of e-waste policies.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets, with a recycling symbol superimposed, illustrating the e-waste challenge.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries globally, with a significant portion still handled by the informal sector.

articles.headline: Indian Air Force Kicks Off 'Gagan Shakti 2025' Mega Exercise, Simulating Multi-Front Conflict
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Air Force (IAF) has commenced its biennial pan-India exercise, 'Gagan Shakti 2025', involving all its commands and assets.
• The mega exercise simulates a high-intensity, multi-front conflict scenario, testing the IAF's readiness, logistical capabilities, and operational doctrines.
• It focuses on long-range missions, air defence, offensive air operations, and jointmanship with other services, including rapid deployment across diverse geographical terrains.
• The exercise aims to validate new technologies, refine strategic concepts, and assess the integration of recent inductions like advanced fighter jets and missile systems.
• 'Gagan Shakti' is a critical demonstration of India's air power capabilities and its commitment to regional security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Defence technology and modernization
prelimsPoints: Indian Air Force (IAF) is the air arm of the Indian Armed Forces.
'Gagan Shakti' is a large-scale, pan-India exercise conducted by the IAF.
The exercise tests various aspects of air warfare, including logistics and command control.
Recent inductions include Rafale fighter jets and S-400 air defence systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of large-scale military exercises like 'Gagan Shakti' in enhancing India's defence preparedness and deterrence capabilities.
Analyze the challenges involved in simulating and managing multi-front conflict scenarios for the Indian Air Force.
Evaluate the role of technological advancements and jointmanship with other services in modernizing India's air power and ensuring national security.
articles.imageDescription: Multiple Indian Air Force fighter jets flying in formation during a military exercise, with contrails visible.
articles.trivia: The first 'Gagan Shakti' exercise was conducted in 2018, involving over 11,000 personnel and 1,100 aircraft.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Forge Green Hydrogen Partnership to Accelerate Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil have announced a strategic partnership focused on accelerating the development and deployment of green hydrogen technologies.
• The collaboration aims to share research and development expertise, co-invest in pilot projects, and establish supply chains for green hydrogen production.
• Both nations, major emerging economies, recognize green hydrogen's potential to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors and achieve climate targets.
• The agreement includes joint efforts to standardize policies and regulations to facilitate international trade and investment in green hydrogen.
• This partnership underscores the growing importance of South-South cooperation in addressing global climate change challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Brazil is a member of BRICS along with India.
Decarbonization refers to the process of reducing carbon emissions.
South-South cooperation involves collaboration among developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of green hydrogen as a clean energy source and its role in achieving India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the significance of the India-Brazil partnership in green hydrogen for accelerating energy transition and fostering South-South cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing a viable green hydrogen economy in India and globally.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, with a stylized green hydrogen molecule in the foreground, symbolizing cooperation in clean energy.
articles.trivia: Brazil is a significant producer of ethanol from sugarcane, a major biofuel, demonstrating its existing leadership in renewable energy.

highlights: Supreme Court mandates 'Green Tribunal Plus' model, expanding NGT's powers for stricter environmental enforcement.
Economic Survey 2024-25 projects robust GDP growth, emphasizing green transition and sustainable development.
India and France enhance cooperation in defence, space, and Indo-Pacific during strategic dialogue.
ISRO successfully tests next-gen cryogenic engine, crucial for India's future deep space missions.
Government unveils 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint, focusing on digital governance and social equity.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Green Tribunal Plus' model recently mandated by the Supreme Court, consider the following statements:
1. It expands the powers and jurisdiction of the National Green Tribunal (NGT).
2. It calls for a dedicated environmental police force to enforce NGT directives.
3. The NGT was established under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Supreme Court's ruling mandates an expanded role for the NGT. Statement 2 is correct: The model includes a call for a dedicated environmental police force. Statement 3 is incorrect: The NGT was established under the National Green Tribunal Act, 2010, not the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986. The 1986 Act provides for the protection and improvement of environment and for matters connected therewith.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is NOT a core focus area identified in the 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint?
(a) Digital Governance
(b) Social Equity
(c) Sustainable Growth
(d) Military Expansionism
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint focuses on transforming India into a developed nation by 2047, emphasizing pillars of digital governance, social equity, sustainable growth, and global leadership. Military Expansionism is not a stated core focus; rather, the vision promotes peace and global leadership through soft power and responsible governance.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. Aadhaar is a foundational component of India's DPI.
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI) facilitates real-time digital payments.
3. India aims to share its DPI framework and expertise with other developing nations.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. Aadhaar provides a unique digital identity, UPI enables real-time payments, and India has expressed its intent to share its successful DPI model with other developing countries to foster digital transformation and financial inclusion globally.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements best describes 'Green Hydrogen'?
(a) Hydrogen produced from natural gas with carbon capture technology.
(b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
(c) Hydrogen produced from coal gasification.
(d) Hydrogen produced as a byproduct of industrial chemical processes.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Green hydrogen is specifically defined as hydrogen produced through the electrolysis of water, powered by renewable energy sources such as solar or wind, resulting in minimal or zero greenhouse gas emissions during its production. Options (a) and (c) refer to 'blue' and 'grey' hydrogen, respectively, which involve fossil fuels.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Gagan Shakti 2025' exercise, recently commenced by the Indian Air Force, is primarily aimed at:
(a) Testing maritime surveillance capabilities in the Indian Ocean.
(b) Simulating a high-intensity, multi-front conflict scenario.
(c) Conducting joint counter-insurgency operations with the Army.
(d) Showcasing India's advanced naval aviation assets.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Simulating a high-intensity, multi-front conflict scenario.
prelimsMocks.explanation: 'Gagan Shakti' is a biennial, pan-India exercise of the Indian Air Force designed to test its readiness, logistical capabilities, and operational doctrines in a large-scale, multi-front conflict simulation, involving all its commands and assets.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's mandate for a 'Green Tribunal Plus' model signifies a proactive judicial approach to environmental governance. Critically analyze the potential benefits and challenges of this expanded role for environmental protection in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC's 'Green Tribunal Plus' model and the existing NGT framework. Discuss benefits such as expedited justice, stricter enforcement, proactive monitoring, and improved environmental compliance. Highlight challenges like judicial overreach, capacity constraints of NGT, inter-agency coordination issues, and potential for conflicts with executive functions. Conclude with measures for effective implementation and balance.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint emphasizes digital governance and social equity. Discuss how leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) can be instrumental in achieving these twin objectives, while also addressing associated challenges.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance. GS3: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it; Science and Technology- developments and their applications.
mainsMocks.approach: Define 'Vision 2047' and the concepts of digital governance and social equity. Explain what DPI is (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) and how it contributes to efficient service delivery, financial inclusion, transparency, and empowerment. Discuss challenges such as digital divide, data privacy, cybersecurity, and the need for digital literacy. Suggest policy measures for equitable access and secure implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The expansion of the PLI scheme to the Advanced Materials sector is a strategic move towards 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'. Examine the potential economic and strategic implications of this policy for India's manufacturing capabilities and its position in global value chains.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth; Investment models; Science and Technology- indigenization of technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the PLI scheme and its expansion to advanced materials. Explain the concept of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'. Discuss economic implications like reduced import dependence, job creation, increased FDI, and export potential. Analyze strategic implications, including self-reliance in critical technologies (e.g., semiconductors, defense), enhanced national security, and integration into high-tech global value chains. Conclude with challenges and the way forward for sustained growth.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 for effective and expeditious disposal of cases relating to environmental protection.
Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser (CEA) and presented annually by the Ministry of Finance.
The Indo-Pacific region is a geopolitical construct encompassing the Indian Ocean and the western and central Pacific Ocean.
Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants at extremely low temperatures, offering high efficiency.
Amrit Kaal refers to the 25-year period from India's 75th Independence Day (2022) to the 100th (2047).
Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) develops and maintains road networks in India's border areas.
The PLI (Production Linked Incentive) scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and make Indian industries globally competitive.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private innovation.
The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of Lokpal for the Union and Lokayukta for States.

GS1.headline: Government Announces New Scheme for Promotion of Traditional Indian Arts and Crafts
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has unveiled a new national scheme aimed at revitalizing and promoting traditional Indian arts and crafts.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, market linkages, and training programs for artisans, particularly those from marginalized communities.
• It emphasizes preserving endangered art forms, encouraging innovation while retaining traditional essence, and creating global platforms for Indian craftsmanship.
• Initiatives include setting up 'Heritage Craft Villages' and collaborating with e-commerce platforms for wider reach and fair remuneration.
• This effort seeks to empower artisans, boost rural economies, and showcase India's rich cultural heritage on the world stage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: India has a diverse range of traditional arts and crafts, many with GI tags.
GI tag is used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation that are due to that origin.
Ministry of Culture is responsible for the preservation and promotion of Indian culture.
Promotion of traditional crafts contributes to rural livelihoods and cultural tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian arts and crafts in preserving cultural heritage and contributing to the rural economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by Indian artisans in the contemporary market and how the new scheme aims to address them.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives and technological platforms in promoting Indian craftsmanship globally and enhancing India's soft power.
GS1.imageDescription: A skilled artisan meticulously crafting a traditional Indian pottery piece, surrounded by colorful finished products.
GS1.trivia: Channapatna toys from Karnataka, Madhubani paintings from Bihar, and Kanchipuram silk sarees from Tamil Nadu are some famous Indian crafts with GI tags.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Mandates 'Green Tribunal Plus' Model for Enhanced Environmental Enforcement
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has directed the Union Government to establish a 'Green Tribunal Plus' model, expanding the powers and jurisdiction of the National Green Tribunal (NGT).
• This new model aims to streamline environmental clearances, expedite dispute resolution, and ensure stricter compliance with environmental laws across states.
• The ruling emphasizes a proactive role for the NGT in monitoring project implementation and imposing penalties for violations, moving beyond a purely adjudicatory function.
• It calls for enhanced technical expertise within the NGT and a dedicated environmental police force to enforce its directives.
• The judgment comes amidst growing concerns over environmental degradation and the slow pace of justice in complex ecological cases.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Environmental protection acts and policies
prelimsPoints: National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 under the National Green Tribunal Act.
NGT has jurisdiction over all civil cases involving substantial questions relating to the environment.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' and 'Precautionary Principle' are fundamental principles applied by the NGT.
The Supreme Court's ruling expands NGT's enforcement and monitoring capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in environmental protection and the challenges of environmental governance in India.
Analyze the implications of the 'Green Tribunal Plus' model for environmental compliance and sustainable development.
Critically evaluate the balance between economic development and environmental conservation in the context of judicial interventions.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration depicting the scales of justice balancing environment and development, with the NGT logo in the background.
GS2.trivia: The NGT was the third such institution in the world, after Australia and New Zealand, and the first developing country to establish a dedicated environmental tribunal.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Enhanced Cooperation in Defence, Space, and Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership and charting new avenues for cooperation.
• Both nations committed to expanding defence industrial collaboration, including co-development and co-production of advanced military platforms.
• Significant progress was made in space cooperation, with agreements on joint satellite missions and sharing of satellite data for climate monitoring.
• The dialogue also focused on enhancing maritime security and stability in the Indo-Pacific region through joint exercises and intelligence sharing.
• Discussions covered global and regional issues, including counter-terrorism, cyber security, and clean energy transition.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership established in 1998.
Key areas of cooperation include defence, space, civil nuclear energy, and counter-terrorism.
France has been a crucial partner for India in its strategic autonomy goals.
The Indo-Pacific strategy is a shared priority for both nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in India's foreign policy and its role in global affairs.
Discuss how enhanced cooperation in defence and space contributes to India's strategic capabilities and national security.
Evaluate the importance of India-France collaboration in maintaining stability and promoting a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific region.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France depicted against a backdrop of a satellite and a fighter jet, symbolizing strategic cooperation.
GS2.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a Strategic Partnership with India, demonstrating a deep trust and convergence of interests.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' Blueprint, Emphasizing Digital Governance and Social Equity
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government today unveiled a comprehensive 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint, outlining India's development trajectory for the next 22 years.
• The vision document focuses on transforming India into a developed nation by 2047, with pillars of digital governance, social equity, sustainable growth, and global leadership.
• Key initiatives include expanding digital public infrastructure, ensuring universal access to quality education and healthcare, and promoting inclusive economic opportunities.
• It also stresses on strengthening democratic institutions, fostering innovation, and enhancing India's soft power globally.
• The blueprint calls for collaborative efforts from states, private sector, and civil society to achieve these ambitious goals.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Governance, Transparency and Accountability
prelimsPoints: Amrit Kaal refers to the 25-year period from India's 75th Independence Day (2022) to the 100th (2047).
The vision document sets long-term goals for India's socio-economic development.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) includes platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker.
Focus on social equity aims to reduce disparities and ensure opportunities for all.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the 'Vision 2047 for Amrit Kaal' blueprint and its potential impact on India's development trajectory.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing digital governance and ensuring social equity across diverse regions of India.
Critically evaluate the role of various stakeholders, including government, private sector, and civil society, in achieving the goals of Vision 2047.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract graphic depicting India's growth trajectory towards 2047, with elements of technology, sustainability, and diverse communities.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Amrit Kaal' was first introduced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2021, signifying a period of great opportunities.

GS2.headline: National Skill Development Mission 2.0 to Focus on Future-Ready Workforce and Green Skills
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced the launch of National Skill Development Mission (NSDM) 2.0.
• This revamped mission aims to equip India's youth with future-ready skills, focusing on emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and data science.
• A significant emphasis will be placed on 'green skills' to support India's transition to a sustainable economy and meet climate goals.
• The mission will integrate vocational training with formal education, ensuring better employability and career progression for skilled individuals.
• It also seeks to foster entrepreneurship and create a robust ecosystem for lifelong learning and skill upgradation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Skill Development Mission was launched in 2015 to create convergence across skill development schemes.
India has a large youth population, presenting a demographic dividend.
Green skills are competencies required to work in green economy sectors or to green existing jobs.
Integration of vocational training with formal education is a key recommendation of NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in harnessing India's demographic dividend and addressing the challenges of unemployment.
Analyze how the focus on future-ready and green skills under NSDM 2.0 can contribute to India's economic growth and sustainable development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in bridging the skill gap and promoting entrepreneurship among youth.
GS2.imageDescription: Diverse group of young people learning various skills, including digital literacy and practical trade, in a modern training center.
GS2.trivia: India aims to skill over 400 million people by 2022 under various skill development programs, though progress remains a challenge.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Stronger Role in Global South Climate Action at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India's representative at the ongoing UN Climate Forum reiterated the nation's commitment to lead climate action for the Global South.
• India pledged enhanced support for technology transfer, capacity building, and climate finance for developing countries.
• The statement called for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments and provide accessible green technologies.
• Emphasis was placed on promoting sustainable lifestyles and advocating for 'climate justice' at international platforms.
• India highlighted its own progress in renewable energy expansion and adaptation strategies, offering to share best practices.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies.
India has been a strong advocate for 'Climate Justice' and 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities'.
UN Climate Forums (COPs) are annual conferences under the UNFCCC.
India's renewable energy capacity has significantly increased in recent years.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in advocating for the concerns of the Global South regarding climate change at international forums.
Analyze the concept of 'climate justice' and 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities' in the context of global climate negotiations.
Evaluate the challenges faced by developing countries in achieving climate goals and the role of international cooperation in addressing them.
GS2.imageDescription: Representatives from various nations gathered at a UN climate conference, with a focus on developing countries.
GS2.trivia: India launched the International Solar Alliance (ISA) in 2015, a treaty-based intergovernmental organization, primarily to promote solar energy among member countries.

GS2.headline: Proposed Amendments to RTI Act Spark Debate on Transparency and Accountability
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government has introduced proposed amendments to the Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005, sparking a nationwide debate.
• Critics argue that certain proposed changes could dilute the independence of Information Commissions and restrict access to crucial government data.
• Proponents, however, contend that the amendments aim to rationalize the functioning of the Act, address misuse, and ensure better alignment with other legal frameworks.
• Civil society organizations and opposition parties have expressed strong concerns, calling for greater public consultation and parliamentary scrutiny.
• The debate highlights the ongoing tension between government transparency and administrative efficiency.
gsSubTopics: Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
Citizens Charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: The Right to Information Act, 2005, allows citizens to request information from public authorities.
The Central Information Commission (CIC) and State Information Commissions (SICs) are the highest appellate bodies.
The Act aims to promote transparency and accountability in the working of every public authority.
Previous amendments to the RTI Act have also faced criticism regarding the independence of Information Commissioners.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Right to Information Act in strengthening democratic governance and ensuring accountability in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the proposed amendments to the RTI Act, considering their potential impact on transparency and citizen's rights.
Suggest measures to balance the need for government efficiency with the principles of transparency and public access to information.
GS2.imageDescription: A hand holding a magnifying glass over a document titled 'RTI Act', symbolizing scrutiny and transparency, with a blurred Parliament building in the background.
GS2.trivia: The RTI Act was enacted after a long and sustained movement led by civil society organizations, with Mazdoor Kisan Shakti Sangathan (MKSS) being a prominent one.

GS2.headline: Lokpal Institution Completes 5 Years, Reviews Progress in Anti-Corruption Efforts
GS2.explainer:
• The Lokpal of India today marked its fifth anniversary, taking stock of its progress in establishing accountability and combating corruption in public administration.
• The institution highlighted key cases investigated, recommendations made for systemic improvements, and the challenges faced in its initial years.
• The Lokpal emphasized the importance of a robust whistleblower protection mechanism and greater public awareness for effective anti-corruption efforts.
• It called for closer coordination with other anti-corruption agencies and timely disposal of cases to enhance its credibility.
• The review underscored the ongoing journey towards transparent and ethical governance in India.
gsSubTopics: Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of Lokpal for the Union and Lokayukta for States.
Lokpal is an anti-corruption authority or body of ombudsman who represents the public interest.
The first Lokpal of India, Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose, was appointed in 2019.
The institution investigates allegations of corruption against public functionaries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Lokpal institution in promoting transparency and combating corruption in India's public administration.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Lokpal in its functioning and suggest measures to enhance its effectiveness and independence.
Evaluate the broader framework of anti-corruption mechanisms in India and how the Lokpal contributes to ethical governance.
GS2.imageDescription: The emblem of the Lokpal of India, symbolizing justice and accountability, against a backdrop of government buildings.
GS2.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman originated in Sweden in 1809. India's Lokpal is modeled on the ombudsman system.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Forge Green Hydrogen Partnership to Accelerate Energy Transition
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil have announced a strategic partnership focused on accelerating the development and deployment of green hydrogen technologies.
• The collaboration aims to share research and development expertise, co-invest in pilot projects, and establish supply chains for green hydrogen production.
• Both nations, major emerging economies, recognize green hydrogen's potential to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors and achieve climate targets.
• The agreement includes joint efforts to standardize policies and regulations to facilitate international trade and investment in green hydrogen.
• This partnership underscores the growing importance of South-South cooperation in addressing global climate change challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Brazil is a member of BRICS along with India.
Decarbonization refers to the process of reducing carbon emissions.
South-South cooperation involves collaboration among developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of green hydrogen as a clean energy source and its role in achieving India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the significance of the India-Brazil partnership in green hydrogen for accelerating energy transition and fostering South-South cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing a viable green hydrogen economy in India and globally.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, with a stylized green hydrogen molecule in the foreground, symbolizing cooperation in clean energy.
GS2.trivia: Brazil is a significant producer of ethanol from sugarcane, a major biofuel, demonstrating its existing leadership in renewable energy.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey 2024-25 Projects Robust Growth Amidst Global Headwinds; Focus on Green Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The Economic Survey 2024-25, tabled ahead of the Union Budget, projects India's GDP growth to remain robust at 7.0-7.5% for the next fiscal year.
• It highlights strong domestic demand, government capital expenditure, and increased private investment as key growth drivers.
• The survey emphasizes the need for a sustained push towards green energy transition and climate-resilient infrastructure to achieve long-term sustainable growth.
• It also points to global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and commodity price volatility as potential risks.
• Recommendations include further reforms in agriculture, labour markets, and a rationalization of subsidies for fiscal consolidation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is presented annually by the Ministry of Finance, typically before the Union Budget.
It provides a detailed review of the economy's performance and policy recommendations.
Key economic indicators like GDP growth, inflation, and fiscal deficit are discussed.
The survey's focus on green transition aligns with India's climate commitments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers and challenges for India's economic growth as highlighted by the Economic Survey 2024-25.
Discuss the importance of green transition and climate-resilient infrastructure in achieving sustainable economic development.
Evaluate the government's economic policies in light of global headwinds and recommend further reforms.
GS3.imageDescription: Infographic showing a rising growth arrow superimposed with green energy symbols, representing economic growth and green transition.
GS3.trivia: The first Economic Survey was presented in 1950-51, and until 1964, it was presented along with the Union Budget.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Cryogenic Engine for Future Deep Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful hot test of its next-generation cryogenic engine, a crucial step for future deep space missions.
• This advanced engine, designated 'Vikas-II', boasts higher thrust-to-weight ratio and improved specific impulse, enhancing payload capacity and mission duration.
• The technology is vital for India's ambitious interplanetary missions, including potential Venus orbiter and advanced lunar exploration programs.
• The successful test signifies India's growing self-reliance in cutting-edge space propulsion technology, reducing dependence on foreign components.
• It also paves the way for heavier launch vehicles capable of deploying multiple satellites into diverse orbits.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants at very low temperatures.
ISRO's GSLV MkIII (LVM3) uses a cryogenic upper stage.
The 'Vikas' engine family is a staple in ISRO's launch vehicles.
Successful testing of new engines is critical for advancing India's space capabilities.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of indigenous cryogenic engine technology for India's space program and its strategic implications.
Discuss how advancements in space propulsion technology contribute to India's aspirations for deep space exploration and international collaboration.
Analyze the role of ISRO in fostering scientific temper and technological innovation in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Diagram of a cryogenic engine undergoing a test fire, with the ISRO logo prominently displayed.
GS3.trivia: India became the sixth nation in the world to develop cryogenic engine technology, a complex and challenging feat in aerospace engineering.

GS3.headline: New National Wetlands Conservation Plan Launched to Combat Degradation and Boost Biodiversity
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a comprehensive National Wetlands Conservation Plan (NWCP) 2.0.
• The plan aims to strengthen the protection and management of wetlands across India, which are crucial for biodiversity, water security, and climate resilience.
• Key features include a national inventory of wetlands, capacity building for local communities, and integration of wetland conservation into regional planning.
• It also focuses on adopting nature-based solutions for wetland restoration and promoting sustainable livelihoods dependent on these ecosystems.
• The initiative is a response to the alarming rate of wetland degradation due to urbanization, pollution, and unsustainable agricultural practices.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year.
Wetlands provide various ecosystem services like water purification, flood control, and habitat for wildlife.
India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands.
National Wetlands Conservation Programme (NWCP) was initiated in 1985-86.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the major threats they face in India.
Analyze the features and potential effectiveness of the new National Wetlands Conservation Plan in achieving its objectives.
Suggest measures to integrate wetland conservation into broader sustainable development goals and enhance community participation.
GS3.imageDescription: Aerial view of a vibrant wetland ecosystem, showcasing diverse flora and fauna, with a small village in the background.
GS3.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar sites, covering a significant area of its wetlands, making it one of the largest networks in Asia.

GS3.headline: India Enhances Border Infrastructure Along Northern Fronts with Advanced Surveillance Systems
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a significant upgrade in border infrastructure along India's northern frontiers, deploying advanced surveillance and communication systems.
• The initiative includes the construction of strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels to facilitate faster troop movement and logistics in challenging terrains.
• New-generation drones, integrated sensor networks, and AI-powered monitoring stations are being installed to enhance real-time situational awareness.
• This move is aimed at bolstering national security, deterring incursions, and improving the operational readiness of armed forces in critical sectors.
• The government reiterates its commitment to safeguarding India's territorial integrity and sovereignty.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: India shares land borders with seven countries, with the northern borders being particularly sensitive.
Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Advanced surveillance systems include drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors.
The push for infrastructure aims to counter challenges posed by difficult terrain and adversarial actions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly along its northern frontiers.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing and maintaining infrastructure in high-altitude and difficult border regions.
Examine how technological advancements in surveillance and communication can enhance border management and deterrence capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Army personnel patrolling a newly constructed border road in a snowy mountainous region, with surveillance equipment visible.
GS3.trivia: The Atal Tunnel (Rohtang Tunnel), inaugurated in 2020, significantly reduced travel time to key border areas in Himachal Pradesh and Ladakh.
